questions.text: You have a reading category but I don't know what you actually read for fun — what kinds of books are you drawn to? Are you more into non-fiction (memoirs, business, science), fiction, or a mix of both? And do you prefer physical books, ebooks, or audiobooks — or does it depend on the situation?

What's your actual gaming habit right now — are you someone who plays regularly (weekly?), more of a sporadic gamer when something catches your interest, or have you been too busy for games lately? And what do you actually like playing — single-player narrative games, competitive multiplayer with friends, casual mobile games, or something else entirely?

When you take time off and plan a trip, are you someone who researches the hell out of everything and has a day-by-day itinerary, or do you prefer to show up and figure it out as you go? Do you like vacations packed with activities and sightseeing, or do you lean more toward lazy days where you just exist in a new place without a plan?

How do you stay informed about the world — news, current events, what's happening in tech/AI? Are you someone who regularly reads news sites, scrolls through Twitter/X, listens to news podcasts, or do you mostly just hear about things through people you know? Any specific publications, newsletters, or sources you trust?

You mentioned photography as a skill you'd love to be great at — do you actually take photos regularly now? Are you someone who carries a camera (even just your phone), do you edit your photos, or do you mostly just capture moments casually without thinking about it as a hobby?
